breaking_news_suggestions.headline: New section: Dome eclipse fails by 220:1 — dome model's own geometry rules out the predicted path
breaking_news_suggestions.summary: §4.2.7 now shows that using the dome's own published Sun/Moon altitudes (5,733 km / 2,534 km) and angular sizes, the Aug 12 2026 eclipse produces a 1.3-km totality circle and 0.9-second totality at any single station — vs the observed 294-km path and 138-second totality. The ratio is ~1:220 in path width and ~1:150 in duration. Two SVG illustrations added.
breaking_news_suggestions.why_newsworthy: Brand new section with independent math verification. Strongest geometric argument against the dome's eclipse prediction — complements the §4.2.2 misstatement argument with hard numbers.
